sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, val DOUBLE, str_val VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, val, str_val) VALUES (1, 2.718, 'text'), (2, NULL, 'test'), (3, 1.0, NULL), (4, 0.0, 'empty');
SELECT id, log(val) << 2 AS shifted_log FROM test_table WHERE val IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, val, str_val, log(COALESCE(val, 1.0)) << LENGTH(COALESCE(str_val, '')) AS complex_expression FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, test_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (test_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO another_table (id, test_id) VALUES (1, 1), (2, NULL), (3, 3);

SELECT t.id, t.val, a.test_id, log(t.val) << (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM another_table WHERE test_id = t.id) AS advanced_calc FROM test_table t JOIN another_table a ON t.id = a.test_id WHERE a.test_id IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t.id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY t.val) AS cnt_val, log(t.val) AS log_val FROM test_table t GROUP BY t.id, log(t.val) HAVING log_val IS NOT NULL ORDER BY cnt_val DESC;
UPDATE test_table SET val = log(COALESCE(val, 1.0)) << 1 WHERE str_val IS NOT NULL;
SELECT log(val) << 3 AS calculated_value FROM test_table WHERE val != 0 AND val IS NOT NULL UNION ALL SELECT log(NULL) AS calculated_value;
SELECT id, log(CAST(val AS STRING)::DOUBLE) << (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM another_table WHERE test_id IS NOT NULL) AS derived_value FROM test_table;
